components.title: Patch Management Policy
components.description: The patch management policy defines the organisation's requirements and processes for identifying, testing, approving, and applying security patches and updates to all IT systems, including operating systems, applications, firmware, and network devices. The policy establishes timeframes for applying patches based on severity and exploitability, and mandates vulnerability scanning to verify patch compliance.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Patch Management Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: As defined in this policy, patches for operating systems of workstations are applied within 48 hours of release when assessed as critical by the vendor or when working exploits exist. The patch management policy specifies the roles, tools, and processes for achieving this timeframe, including automated deployment via Intune or WSUS and an emergency change process for out-of-cycle critical patches.

As defined in this policy, patches for applications (other than the high-risk application set) are applied within one month of release. The patch management policy specifies the application inventory review process, patch identification via vendor advisories and vulnerability scanning, and the standard change process for monthly patching cycles.

As defined in this policy, a vulnerability scanner is used at least weekly to identify missing patches for high-risk applications (browsers, email clients, Microsoft Office, PDF readers, security products). The policy specifies the scanning tool, scan schedule, responsible team, and the escalation process for patch compliance failures.

As defined in this policy, a vulnerability scanner is used at least fortnightly to identify missing operating system patches on workstations and non-internet-facing servers. The policy mandates scan coverage targets and a process for reporting on and remediating patch compliance gaps.

components.title: Windows 11 — Patch Management
components.description: Windows 11 is the standard workstation operating system. Patching is managed via Windows Update for Business (WUfB) and Microsoft Intune. Patch compliance is verified via Tenable Nessus vulnerability scans run fortnightly from the central scan server.
components.purpose: Apply and verify security patches on all Windows 11 workstations.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Windows 11 — Patch Management.

implemented-requirements.description: Security patches assessed as critical, or where working exploits exist, are deployed to Windows 11 workstations within 48 hours via Windows Update for Business configured in the Semi-Annual Channel with accelerated deadline policy.
All other security patches for the Windows 11 operating system are deployed within one month via the standard monthly Patch Tuesday deployment ring.

A vulnerability scanner (Tenable Nessus) is used at least weekly to identify missing patches for high-risk applications on Windows 11 workstations, including web browsers, Microsoft Office, and PDF readers.

A vulnerability scanner is run at least fortnightly to identify missing OS-level patches on Windows 11 workstations. Results are reviewed by the security team and outstanding patches are escalated within 5 business days.

components.title: Vulnerability Scanner — Tenable Nessus
components.description: Tenable Nessus Professional is deployed as the vulnerability scanner for all on-premises endpoints and servers. Authenticated scans use a domain service account with local admin rights to ensure accurate patch and configuration findings.
components.purpose: Identify missing patches and security misconfigurations across all endpoints.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Vulnerability Scanner — Tenable Nessus.

implemented-requirements.description: Nessus performs authenticated weekly scans targeting high-risk applications (browsers, email clients, Microsoft Office, PDF readers) on all workstations. Critical findings are reviewed within 24 hours.

Nessus performs authenticated fortnightly scans of all workstation and server operating systems to identify missing patches. Scan results are exported to the SIEM and summarised in the monthly patch compliance report.

Nessus scan results include end-of-life (EOL) detection. Applications and operating systems flagged as no longer receiving vendor security support are escalated to the system owner for removal or exception approval.

capabilities.name: Patch Management
capabilities.description: The organisation's patch management capability combines policy, automated patch deployment, and vulnerability scanning to ensure security patches are applied within required timeframes. Windows Update for Business delivers patches to workstations while Tenable Nessus verifies compliance across all endpoints.

incorporates-components.description: Defines the patch management policy and required timeframes.
Applies patches to workstations and verifies compliance via WUfB.
Identifies missing patches through authenticated vulnerability scanning.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for capability: Patch Management. Includes contributions from member components (identified by source-component-uuid prop) and capability-level synthesis where present.
